what_it_does: Implements a file-discovered registry for MARK-TYPE vocabulary entries: it loads one Python module per mark-type from configured directories, validates each module's MARK_TYPE dict, and exposes the resulting MarkType objects through a dict-like MarkTypeRegistry.
observations: Each mark-type is declared in its own file (mark_types/<id>.py) as a module-level MARK_TYPE dict.
Validation is fail-loud: bad id, id/filename mismatch, missing value_shape, unknown fields, or wrong types raise immediately.
MarkType is a dataclass holding the declared spec plus typed accessors for direction and desc.
MarkTypeRegistry mirrors the discovery/rediscovery/register pattern used by other corpus registries (projections, roles, nodes).
It provides subtractive() to list mark-types with direction=='subtract' and as_records() to return the raw declared specs.
Direction defaults to 'surface' and is described as an open vocab, with 'subtract' representing the inversion/denoising semantics.
The file is self-contained and explicitly notes it is not yet sharing a base class with the other registry implementations.
standards: The 'subtract' direction encodes fingerprint-subtraction/denoising semantics as data rather than hardcoded logic.
Fail-loud validation makes malformed vocabulary entries surface immediately at discovery time.
Registry-is-truth: mark-passes are expected to draw mark_type values from as_records().
conventions: Follows the established file-per-entry, importlib-based registry pattern.
Heavy docstring commentary explaining design rationale and future wiring.
Open vocab strings for value_shape and direction instead of Python enums.
concerns: Duplicate ids silently overwrite each other in register() because no check is performed.
No directory recursion; only top-level .py files are discovered.
DIRECTIONS tuple is defined but not used for validation, so any non-empty string direction is accepted.
The registry pattern is duplicated here rather than shared, which the file acknowledges as future work.
notes: A self-contained, file-discovered vocabulary registry for mark-types that treats adding a new disposition kind as dropping a file rather than editing a central dictionary.
questions: Where are the actual mark_types/ directory entries located and what ids do they define?
What component instantiates MarkTypeRegistry and passes the directory list?
Which mark-pass consumes as_records() and how does it guarantee mark_type values come from the registry?
Is a shared registry base class planned, and if so which file will own it?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it provides a file-discovered, validated, read-only registry for mark-type vocabulary.
intention_signals: The file is trying to make the set of dispositions a mark-pass can write as auditable, operator-overridable, and extensible data, while preventing silent drift from typos or mismatched ids.
novelty: The explicit 'direction' field that encodes whether a mark surfaces signal or subtracts noise, turning a domain-specific denoising discipline into registry metadata.
contribution: Provides a validated, file-discovered MarkType/MarkTypeRegistry substrate that lets new mark kinds be added without code changes and lets consumers query the vocabulary as plain records.
summary_for_embedding: runtime/mark_types.py defines a file-discovered, fail-loud-validated registry for mark-type vocabulary entries used by mark-passes to stamp dispositions on corpus units.
intention_for_embedding: To make mark-type vocabulary a create-a-file extensible data layer rather than a hardcoded Python dictionary, supporting surfacing and subtractive denoising semantics.

symbols.does: Dataclass representing one discovered mark-type row.
symbols.description: Holds the mark-type id, value_shape, and the full declared spec; exposes typed direction and desc properties with a default direction of 'surface'.

symbols.does: Property returning the mark-type direction, defaulting to 'surface'.
symbols.description: Reads the declared direction from the spec and falls back to 'surface', encoding whether the mark is positive signal or subtractive noise.

symbols.does: Property returning the optional human-readable description.
symbols.description: Returns the declared 'desc' field from the spec, or None if absent.

symbols.does: Loads a single .py file as a module by filename.
symbols.description: Uses importlib.util.spec_from_file_location to execute the module and returns its basename (without extension) and the module object.

symbols.does: Validates a MARK_TYPE dict and constructs a MarkType.
symbols.description: Checks that the declaration is a dict, has a string id matching the filename, contains only known fields, has a non-empty string value_shape, and has a valid direction if present; raises on any failure.

symbols.does: File-discovered registry container for MarkType objects.
symbols.description: Provides discovery, rediscovery, registration, and read-only accessors, plus dict-like lookup over validated mark-type entries.
Creates an empty dict that will hold validated MarkType instances keyed by id.

symbols.does: Scans directories and registers valid mark-type modules.
symbols.description: Iterates sorted .py files in each directory, skips underscore-prefixed files, loads modules, and registers those declaring MARK_TYPE.
Empties the registry and calls discover() again to support reloading.

symbols.does: Validates and stores a mark-type declaration.
symbols.description: Passes the declaration through _build_mark_type and assigns the resulting MarkType into the internal dict by module name.

symbols.does: Returns all mark-types whose direction is 'subtract'.
symbols.description: Filters the registry for subtractive/denoising mark-types and returns them sorted by id.

symbols.does: Returns the full mark-type set as plain declared dicts.
symbols.description: Produces a list of the original MARK_TYPE specs in id-sorted order for registry-is-truth consumers.

symbols.does: Dict-like lookup returning a MarkType by id.
symbols.description: Raises KeyError for unknown ids, consistent with standard mapping behavior.

symbols.does: Dict-like membership test for an id.
symbols.description: Returns True if the id is present in the registry.

symbols.does: Iterates over the registered mark-type ids.
symbols.description: Yields keys from the internal mark_types dictionary.

symbols.does: Returns the count of registered mark-types.
symbols.description: Reports the size of the internal mark_types dictionary.
Returns the MarkType for the given id, or the provided default if absent.
